_comment: AI Library — curated books and articles for academics. Affiliate links point to Amazon (books) or publisher/open-access (articles). Replace placeholder refs with real affiliate tags when accounts are set up.

books.title: Life 3.0: Being Human in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
books.author: Max Tegmark
books.description: A sweeping exploration of how AI will transform every aspect of society — from jobs and law to war and consciousness. Essential reading for anyone entering the AI field.

books.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
books.author: Stuart Russell & Peter Norvig
books.description: The definitive AI textbook used in over 1,500 universities. Covers search, knowledge, planning, learning, perception, and robotics with rigorous depth.

books.title: Deep Learning
books.author: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio & Aaron Courville
books.description: The authoritative textbook on deep learning covering linear algebra, probability, numerical computation, and modern deep network architectures.

books.title: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
books.author: Aurelien Geron
books.description: The most practical ML book available. Takes you from zero to building production-ready models with Python, with clear code examples throughout.

books.title: Atlas of AI: Power, Politics, and the Planetary Costs of Artificial Intelligence
books.author: Kate Crawford
books.description: A revelatory investigation into the material and political costs of AI — from lithium mines to data labourers. Critical for understanding AI's societal impact.

books.title: Human Compatible: Artificial Intelligence and the Problem of Control
books.author: Stuart Russell
books.description: A leading AI researcher makes the case that we need to fundamentally rethink how we build AI systems to ensure they remain beneficial to humanity.

books.title: The Prompt Engineering Guide
books.author: DAIR.AI
books.description: A comprehensive, regularly updated guide to prompt engineering techniques for LLMs. Covers zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, and advanced prompting strategies.

books.title: Ace the Data Science Interview
books.author: Nick Singh & Kevin Huo
books.description: 201 real interview questions from top tech companies. Covers statistics, ML, SQL, product sense, and case studies — ideal for academics transitioning to industry.

articles.title: Attention Is All You Need
articles.description: The paper that introduced the Transformer architecture — the foundation of GPT, BERT, and virtually every modern language model.

articles.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
articles.description: Introduced bidirectional pre-training for language models, revolutionising NLP benchmarks and spawning an entire family of models.

articles.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
articles.description: OpenAI's technical report on GPT-4 — a large multimodal model that exhibits human-level performance on various professional and academic benchmarks.

articles.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
articles.description: Anthropic's approach to training AI systems using a set of principles (a 'constitution') rather than purely human feedback — a key paper in AI alignment.

articles.title: Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early experiments with GPT-4
articles.description: Microsoft Research's extensive evaluation of GPT-4's capabilities across mathematics, coding, vision, and reasoning — arguing it shows 'sparks' of AGI.

articles.title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
articles.description: A critical examination of the risks of large language models — including environmental costs, bias, and the illusion of understanding. Essential counterpoint reading.
